title: hieracium tendinum subsp. tinctilingua
intro: Hieracium tendinum subsp. tinctilingua, also known as hawkweed or mouse-ear hawkweed, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium tendinum subsp. tinctilingua include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Hieracium tendinum subsp. tinctilingua can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in salads or as a garnish.
